# ⚡ MetaHarness × SuperInstance — Plug-and-Play Fleet Integration

## The Big Idea

Metaharness (forked from ruvnet/agent-harness-generator) generates custom AI agent harnesses.
SuperInstance has a superior **git-agent fleet architecture** (baton-system, I2I, GC, tiered repos, Level-4 agent activation).

**Bridge**: Every harness generated by our fork automatically gets SuperInstance fleet protocols
plugged in — A2A orchestration, I2I shared state, Git-Agent rules, GC integration, fleet awareness.

## What We're Building

3 layers:

### Layer 1: Enhanced `host-openclaw` Adapter (done — needs upgrading)
The existing adapter generates MCP configs and skills. We extend it to emit:
- `AGENTS.md` — the fleet constitution (from baton-system template)
- `.gcconfig` — GC manifest for the generated harness
- `FLEET_PROTOCOL.md` — how this harness participates in the fleet
- `i2i-vessel/` — local vessel directory structure
- `fleet-scout.sh` — fleet monitoring script (ported from pincher)

### Layer 2: `@superinstance/fleet-kit` — The Glue Library
A new npm package that any generated harness can import:
- `A2A` client — spawn/manage subagents via OpenClaw sessions API
- `I2I` client — read/write bottles to vessel (port 8796 harbor protocol)
- `GC` reporter — report disk/RAM/load to gc-intelligent ledger
- `FleetRegistry` — register/unregister with fleet-oracle2's registry
- `PulseMetrics` — push conservation metrics to conservation-meter

### Layer 3: `@superinstance/fleet-kit-plugin` — Metaharness Plugin
A generator plugin that:
- Adds SuperInstance fleet agents to the harness (fleet-coordinator, gc-agent, I2I-relay)
- Injects fleet protocol skills into the skill manifest
- Configures MCP servers pointing at the fleet services
- Sets up cron jobs for heartbeat and GC reporting

## Implementation Order

1. Upgrade `host-openclaw` — add AGENTS.md, .gcconfig, fleet-scout.sh to generated output
2. Build `@superinstance/fleet-kit` — TypeScript SDK for fleet protocols
3. Add `@superinstance/fleet-kit-plugin` — Metaharness plugin that layers fleet integration
4. Register as release target: every `npx create-agent-harness` can `--with-fleet`
5. Document the integration with examples: `npx create-agent-harness my-bot --with-fleet --host openclaw`

## Fleet Protocol Files Each Harness Gets

```
my-bot/
├── AGENTS.md           # Fleet constitution (READ THIS FIRST)
├── .gcconfig           # GC policy manifest
├── FLEET_PROTOCOL.md   # How this harness participates
├── i2i-vessel/
│   ├── bottles/        # Shared state bottles
│   └── SESSION-STATE.md
├── scripts/
│   ├── fleet-scout.sh  # Ping fleet-oracle2
│   └── gc-self-audit.sh # Local GC reporting
├── install-fleet.sh    # Idempotent fleet registration
└── ...
```

## What Changes from Upstream

| Upstream | SuperInstance Fork |
|----------|-------------------|
| CLI scaffold only | CLI scaffold + fleet protocols |
| MCP servers only | MCP + I2I vessels + A2A subagents |
| Standalone harness | Fleet-aware participant |
| npm publish | npm publish + fleet registration |
| CI: test+lint | CI: test+lint+GC self-audit |
